description_long: This object is a horseshoe crab, featuring a broad, dome-shaped carapace that is smooth and brownish-tan in color. The carapace covers the main body and tapers into a long, rigid, pointed tail spine (telson) extending straight from the rear. The surface of the shell is slightly glossy with subtle textural ridges and a hard, chitinous appearance. The underside and legs are not visible, but the overall form is low and flattened, with the front edge of the carapace gently curved and the rear narrowing to the tail. No claws or antennae are visible, and the eyes are not apparent from these views. The object rests on a flat, gray, stone-like surface, emphasizing its natural, aquatic origin.
description: A hard-shelled, brown horseshoe crab with a smooth, domed carapace and a long, pointed tail spine, resting on a stone surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the domed carapace and the full length of the straight, pointed tail spine.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the symmetrical tapering of the carapace and the central alignment of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the carapace's curvature and tail visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the broad, rounded front edge of the carapace and the absence of visible eyes or appendages.
